methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sunfire, a Series E climate and energy company founded by Nils Aldag, has raised $315M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a fragmented peer universe of 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. The private peer set spans adjacent funding stages and valuation tiers but lacks sufficient same-stage, same-tier clustering to support a robust peer-median methodology.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation tool. Using total capital raised ($315M) as a baseline valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E progression yields an implied valuation of $441M. This reflects standard expansion-stage advancement patterns in frontier technology.

The peer-median approach is not applicable; while 21 same-sector Climate & Energy peers exist broadly, filtering for comparable stage and valuation tier yields fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical confidence. A full-sector median would introduce excessive noise. Accordingly, the triangulated valuation range is anchored to the single applicable method: $375M (low), $441M (median), and $507M (high). Key uncertainties center on the durability of the total-raised proxy at Series E maturity, the appropriate step-up multiplier given Sunfire's specific technology risk profile, and peer comparability within an otherwise heterogeneous climate tech landscape.
